rows.name: Standing Light Punch
rows.notes: Good neutral buffer into 5LP~LP Target Combo (requires at least a Counter-hit to combo); causes air reset if juggled into

raw.notes: Good neutral buffer into 5LP~LP Target Combo (requires at least a Counter-hit to combo); causes air reset if juggled into

rows.name: Standing Medium Punch
rows.notes: 2f extra recovery on whiff; great hitbox and a strong buffer into 5MP~MP Target Combo

raw.notes: 2f extra recovery on whiff; great hitbox and a strong buffer into 5MP~MP Target Combo

rows.name: Standing Heavy Punch
rows.notes: Can hold button 21f for improved properties; strong whiff punish tool

raw.notes: Can hold button 21f for improved properties; strong whiff punish tool

rows.name: Marisa Style (HP)
rows.notes: 1f extra recovery on whiff; releasing the button on frames 7-17 results in uncharged version with 5f startup; great range and frame advantage for follow-up combos and pressure; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

raw.notes: 1f extra recovery on whiff; releasing the button on frames 7-17 results in uncharged version with 5f startup; great range and frame advantage for follow-up combos and pressure; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

rows.name: Standing Light Kick
rows.notes: At farther ranges, can only combo into SA2; special cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame; DR cancel is delayed until after 3rd recovery frame

raw.notes: At farther ranges, can only combo into SA2; special cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame; DR cancel is delayed until after 3rd recovery frame

rows.name: Standing Medium Kick
rows.notes: Good poke and Drive Rush starter

raw.notes: Good poke and Drive Rush starter

rows.name: Standing Heavy Kick
rows.notes: Can hold button 17f for improved properties; solid poke with good hitbox priority; Punish Counter puts opponent into free juggle state; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles

raw.notes: Can hold button 17f for improved properties; solid poke with good hitbox priority; Punish Counter puts opponent into free juggle state; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles

rows.name: Marisa Style (HK)
rows.notes: Releasing the button on frames 5-13 results in uncharged version with 10f startup; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; Punish Counter causes free juggle state

raw.notes: Releasing the button on frames 5-13 results in uncharged version with 10f startup; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; Punish Counter causes free juggle state

rows.name: Crouching Light Punch
rows.notes: Chains into 2LP/2LK; Marisa's only 4f normal; special cancel is delayed until after active frames; DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame

raw.notes: Chains into 2LP/2LK; Marisa's only 4f normal; special cancel is delayed until after active frames; DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame

rows.name: Crouching Medium Punch
rows.notes: Long range for a cancelable 2MP (but slower than average startup); topmost hurtboxes on frames 8-15 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot)

raw.notes: Long range for a cancelable 2MP (but slower than average startup); topmost hurtboxes on frames 8-15 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot)

rows.name: Crouching Heavy Punch
rows.notes: Can hold button 17f for improved properties; slow but solid anti-air; cannot hit cross-up; only first 2 active frames hit grounded opponents; active frames 3-5 only hit airborne opponents; puts opponent into limited juggle state on hit (higher launch vs. airborne, and much higher on Punish Counter)

raw.notes: Can hold button 17f for improved properties; slow but solid anti-air; cannot hit cross-up; only first 2 active frames hit grounded opponents; active frames 3-5 only hit airborne opponents; puts opponent into limited juggle state on hit (higher launch vs. airborne, and much higher on Punish Counter)

rows.name: Crouching Heavy Punch (Hold)
rows.notes: Launches opponent into limited juggle state on hit, and much higher launch on Punish Counter; useful for continuing juggles; cannot hit cross-up and too slow to realistically anti-air; only first 2 active frames hit grounded opponents; active frames 3-6 only hit airborne opponents; releasing the button on frames 5-16 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; only Super cancelable on the first 2 active frames; can always combo into a fully charged SA1 for incredible 1-bar damage; hitstop is 2 frames longer on hit than on block for easier cancel hitconfirms

raw.notes: Launches opponent into limited juggle state on hit, and much higher launch on Punish Counter; useful for continuing juggles; cannot hit cross-up and too slow to realistically anti-air; only first 2 active frames hit grounded opponents; active frames 3-6 only hit airborne opponents; releasing the button on frames 5-16 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; only Super cancelable on the first 2 active frames; can always combo into a fully charged SA1 for incredible 1-bar damage; hitstop is 2 frames longer on hit than on block for easier cancel hitconfirms

rows.name: Crouching Light Kick
rows.range_notes: Terrible punish, but the only 5-frame option when outside of 2LP range
rows.notes: Chains into 2LP/2LK

raw.rangeNotes: ''Terrible punish, but the only 5-frame option when outside of 2LP range''
raw.notes: Chains into 2LP/2LK

rows.name: Crouching Medium Kick
rows.notes: 1f extra recovery on whiff/block; solid low poke with good range and hitbox priority; strong Drive Rush starter that links into 4HP or 5MP~MP at close range, 2MP at mid range, or 6MP from farther out; topmost hurtboxes on frames 9-16 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot but becomes vulnerable on frame 17~)

raw.notes: 1f extra recovery on whiff/block; solid low poke with good range and hitbox priority; strong Drive Rush starter that links into 4HP or 5MP~MP at close range, 2MP at mid range, or 6MP from farther out; topmost hurtboxes on frames 9-16 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot but becomes vulnerable on frame 17~)

rows.name: Gaia Bash
rows.notes: Can hold button 20f for improved properties; has juggle potential; topmost hurtboxes on frames 1-7 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot)

raw.notes: Can hold button 20f for improved properties; has juggle potential; topmost hurtboxes on frames 1-7 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot)

rows.name: Gaia Bash (Hold)
rows.notes: 3f extra recovery on whiff; has juggle potential and keeps the opponent in a limited juggle state (e.g. corner OTG bounce > 2[HK] > SA2); releasing the button on frames 7-15 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; topmost hurtboxes on frames 1-7 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot but becomes vulnerable on frame 8~)

raw.notes: 3f extra recovery on whiff; has juggle potential and keeps the opponent in a limited juggle state (e.g. corner OTG bounce > 2[HK] > SA2); releasing the button on frames 7-15 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; topmost hurtboxes on frames 1-7 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot but becomes vulnerable on frame 8~)

rows.name: Novacula 1
rows.notes: Strong forward movement and can link into light normals after Counter-hit, Punish Counter, or Drive Rush; has 2 hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-ups; useful link option out of DR~2MK due to its range

raw.notes: Strong forward movement and can link into light normals after Counter-hit, Punish Counter, or Drive Rush; has 2 hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-ups; useful link option out of DR~2MK due to its range

rows.name: Magna Bunker
rows.notes: 3f extra recovery on whiff; can hold button 17f for improved properties; causes air reset when juggled into without Drive Rush; short range but important in combos and pressure; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame

raw.notes: 3f extra recovery on whiff; can hold button 17f for improved properties; causes air reset when juggled into without Drive Rush; short range but important in combos and pressure; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame

rows.name: Magna Bunker (Hold)
rows.notes: 5f extra recovery on whiff; 2nd active frame is omitted on hit/block (when combined with the extra whiff recovery, the total frame count is 6f longer on whiff compared to hit/block); releasing the button on frames 5-16 results in uncharged version with 3f startup; short range but excellent combo and pressure tool; meaty connect does not give extra frame advantage; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

raw.notes: 5f extra recovery on whiff; 2nd active frame is omitted on hit/block (when combined with the extra whiff recovery, the total frame count is 6f longer on whiff compared to hit/block); releasing the button on frames 5-16 results in uncharged version with 3f startup; short range but excellent combo and pressure tool; meaty connect does not give extra frame advantage; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

rows.name: Malleus Breaker 1
rows.notes: Can hold button 17f for improved propertied; has a Target Combo follow-up; causes a short OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents

raw.notes: Can hold button 17f for improved propertied; has a Target Combo follow-up; causes a short OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents

rows.name: Malleus Breaker 1 (Hold)
rows.notes: Slow overhead that can combo on hit or give pressure on block; releasing the button on frames 7-13 results in uncharged version with 14f startup; causes a short OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents

raw.notes: Slow overhead that can combo on hit or give pressure on block; releasing the button on frames 7-13 results in uncharged version with 14f startup; causes a short OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents

rows.name: Falx Crusher 1
rows.notes: Can hold button 21f for improved properties; has a hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-up; forces stand on hit

raw.notes: Can hold button 21f for improved properties; has a hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-up; forces stand on hit

rows.name: Falx Crusher 1 (Hold)
rows.notes: Releasing the button on frames 7-14 results in uncharged version with 7f startup (frames 15-20: 4f startup); has a hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-up; forces stand on hit; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; more range than uncharged version with excellent follow-up combos or block pressure; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 3rd active frame

raw.notes: Releasing the button on frames 7-14 results in uncharged version with 7f startup (frames 15-20: 4f startup); has a hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-up; forces stand on hit; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; more range than uncharged version with excellent follow-up combos or block pressure; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 3rd active frame

rows.name: Caelum Arc
rows.notes: Can hit Cross-up; can hold button 20f for improved properties; causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; lower body projectile invuln 9f until land except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

raw.notes: Can hit Cross-up; can hold button 20f for improved properties; causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; lower body projectile invuln 9f until land except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

rows.name: Caelum Arc (Hold)
rows.notes: Can hit Cross-up; releasing the button on frames 5-19 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; causes spike knockdown; lower body projectile invuln 28f until land except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

raw.notes: Can hit Cross-up; releasing the button on frames 5-19 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; causes spike knockdown; lower body projectile invuln 28f until land except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

rows.name: Light Two Hitter
rows.notes: Requires Counter-hit, Punish Counter, or Drive Rush 5LP to combo; 3f gap on block between hits that allows for a frame trap

raw.notes: Requires Counter-hit, Punish Counter, or Drive Rush 5LP to combo; 3f gap on block between hits that allows for a frame trap

rows.name: Medium Two Hitter
rows.notes: Great pressure and bully tool from 5MP; true blockstring between hits; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame

raw.notes: Great pressure and bully tool from 5MP; true blockstring between hits; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame

rows.name: Heavy Two Hitter
rows.notes: () refers to scaled damage after 5HP combo starter; 6f gap between hits on block (1f gap if starting from 5[HP]); grants a safe jump near the corner on a grounded hit or low-height juggle

raw.notes: () refers to scaled damage after 5HP combo starter; 6f gap between hits on block (1f gap if starting from 5[HP]); grants a safe jump near the corner on a grounded hit or low-height juggle

rows.name: Novacula Swipe
rows.notes: Low follow-up; true blockstring from 6MP, so an opponent mashing a crouching button after blocked 6MP will not be frame trapped; useful confirm if 6MP hits crouching opponent

raw.notes: Low follow-up; true blockstring from 6MP, so an opponent mashing a crouching button after blocked 6MP will not be frame trapped; useful confirm if 6MP hits crouching opponent

rows.name: Novacula Thrust
rows.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents; if the opponent is standing, this ender is preferable for its corner carry and Super cancelability; Super cancel is delayed until after 3rd active frame

raw.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents; if the opponent is standing, this ender is preferable for its corner carry and Super cancelability; Super cancel is delayed until after 3rd active frame

rows.name: Malleus Breaker 2
rows.notes: Gives a risky 2nd overhead after 3HP; true blockstring, so opponent can safely mash a standing normal after blocking 3HP without being frame trapped or hit by the overhead; can be juggled into (KD +31); can whiff at longer ranges of 3HP (much more unsafe)

raw.notes: Gives a risky 2nd overhead after 3HP; true blockstring, so opponent can safely mash a standing normal after blocking 3HP without being frame trapped or hit by the overhead; can be juggled into (KD +31); can whiff at longer ranges of 3HP (much more unsafe)

rows.name: Falx Crusher 2
rows.notes: Hitconfirmable ender after 6HK; delaying the input can create a frame trap up to 3f on block (no gap if starting from 6[HK]); puts opponent into limited juggle state; extremely powerful when juggled into (does not increase juggle count at all from DR~6HK)

raw.notes: Hitconfirmable ender after 6HK; delaying the input can create a frame trap up to 3f on block (no gap if starting from 6[HK]); puts opponent into limited juggle state; extremely powerful when juggled into (does not increase juggle count at all from DR~6HK)

rows.name: Volare Combo
rows.notes: Spike KD vs. airborne opponents

raw.notes: Spike KD vs. airborne opponents

rows.name: Mounted Grace
rows.notes: Can use Drive Rush for a corner throw loop with 2f timing on standard characters; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

raw.notes: Can use Drive Rush for a corner throw loop with 2f timing on standard characters; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

rows.name: Ponte Milvio
rows.notes: Side switch; after throwing opponent into corner, can Drive Rush for strong strike/throw mixup; DR~5[HP] trades with reversal 4f normals for a high-damage trade combo; meaty options are much weaker after normal dash (no throw, limited conversions on hit); a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

raw.notes: Side switch; after throwing opponent into corner, can Drive Rush for strong strike/throw mixup; DR~5[HP] trades with reversal 4f normals for a high-damage trade combo; meaty options are much weaker after normal dash (no throw, limited conversions on hit); a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

rows.name: Secutor
rows.notes: See Drive Impact. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.

raw.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Impact|Drive Impact]]. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.

rows.name: Love Tap (Block)
rows.notes: Performed by inputting 6HPHK during blockstun; 5f extra recovery on hit; freezes the screen for 4f during startup; 4f less hitstop on block compared to most Drive Reversals (changes the punish timing); see Drive Reversal.

raw.notes: Performed by inputting 6HPHK during blockstun; 5f extra recovery on hit; freezes the screen for 4f during startup; 4f less hitstop on block compared to most Drive Reversals (changes the punish timing); see [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Reversal|Drive Reversal]].

rows.name: Love Tap (Recovery)
rows.notes: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze except for a 12f freeze when interacting with the opponent's throw hitbox (this also refunds 1 Drive bar after punishing the throw); 4f less hitstop on block compared to most Drive Reversals (changes the punish timing); see Drive Reversal.

raw.notes: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze except for a 12f freeze when interacting with the opponent's throw hitbox (this also refunds 1 Drive bar after punishing the throw); 4f less hitstop on block compared to most Drive Reversals (changes the punish timing); see [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Reversal|Drive Reversal]].

rows.name: Drive Parry
rows.notes: See Drive Parry. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

raw.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Parry|Drive Parry]]. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

rows.name: Parry Drive Rush
rows.notes: See Drive Rush; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.544 (min/throw), 1.903 (min/block), 3.081 (max/final DR frame)

raw.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Rush|Drive Rush]]; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.544 (min/throw), 1.903 (min/block), 3.081 (max/final DR frame)

rows.name: Drive Rush Cancel
rows.notes: See Drive Rush; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

raw.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Rush|Drive Rush]]; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

rows.name: Phalanx
rows.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; hits 1f later vs. crouching opponents; leaves Marisa close enough to frame trap with 5LP~LP on block or use 5MP~MP without causing an air reset; can set up trade combos with 4f reversals using 5MP vs. stand block or 4HP vs. crouch block

raw.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; hits 1f later vs. crouching opponents; leaves Marisa close enough to frame trap with 5LP~LP on block or use 5MP~MP without causing an air reset; can set up trade combos with 4f reversals using 5MP vs. stand block or 4HP vs. crouch block

rows.name: Phalanx
rows.notes: Sends opponent tumbling backward into OTG state; if opponent hits the corner, a low-height Wall Splat occurs (opponent is grounded and throwable, but strikes put them into juggle state); cannot hit cross-up; hits 1f later vs. crouching opponents; can set up trade combos with 4f reversals using 4HP vs. stand block or 6MP vs. crouch block

raw.notes: Sends opponent tumbling backward into OTG state; if opponent hits the corner, a low-height Wall Splat occurs (opponent is grounded and throwable, but strikes put them into juggle state); cannot hit cross-up; hits 1f later vs. crouching opponents; can set up trade combos with 4f reversals using 4HP vs. stand block or 6MP vs. crouch block

rows.name: Quadriga
rows.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; ~50% screen length

raw.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; ~50% screen length

rows.name: Quadriga
rows.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; ~60% screen length

raw.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; ~60% screen length

rows.name: Quadriga
rows.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; ~70% screen length

raw.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; ~70% screen length

rows.name: Quadriga
rows.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; ~65% screen length; Wall Splat on hit; KD Advantage increases when farther from the wall; significant pushback on block makes it hard to punish when spaced out

raw.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; ~65% screen length; Wall Splat on hit; KD Advantage increases when farther from the wall; significant pushback on block makes it hard to punish when spaced out

rows.name: Scutum
rows.notes: Stance with 1-hit armor can be extended an extra 90f by holding the button, followed by 26 unarmored recovery frames; no armor on lower half of body; cancelable into follow-ups after 8f; stance cannot be canceled into from other attacks; absorbing an attack automatically triggers counterattack follow-up; Marisa takes a fixed 6f hitstop when absorbing attacks (causing the counterattack to often land as a Counter-hit rather than a Punish Counter against medium/heavy attacks)

raw.notes: Stance with 1-hit armor can be extended an extra 90f by holding the button, followed by 26 unarmored recovery frames; no armor on lower half of body; cancelable into follow-ups after 8f; stance cannot be canceled into from other attacks; absorbing an attack automatically triggers counterattack follow-up; Marisa takes a fixed 6f hitstop when absorbing attacks (causing the counterattack to often land as a Counter-hit rather than a Punish Counter against medium/heavy attacks)

rows.name: Scutum
rows.notes: Stance with 2-hit full-body armor can be extended an extra 30f by holding a kick button, followed by 29 unarmored recovery frames; cancelable into follow-ups after 8f; stance cannot be canceled into from other attacks; absorbing an attack automatically triggers counterattack follow-up; Marisa takes a fixed 6f hitstop when absorbing attacks (causing the counterattack to often land as a Counter-hit rather than a Punish Counter against medium/heavy attacks)

raw.notes: Stance with 2-hit full-body armor can be extended an extra 30f by holding a kick button, followed by 29 unarmored recovery frames; cancelable into follow-ups after 8f; stance cannot be canceled into from other attacks; absorbing an attack automatically triggers counterattack follow-up; Marisa takes a fixed 6f hitstop when absorbing attacks (causing the counterattack to often land as a Counter-hit rather than a Punish Counter against medium/heavy attacks)

rows.name: Scutum (Counter)
rows.notes: Automatically triggers counterattack after 214K absorbs attack; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; can hold button to remain in Scutum stance after counterattack; hit advantage depends on attack that is absorbed; Marisa's short 6f hitstop causes attack to land as a +1 Counter-hit against most Medium/Heavy normals, while landing as a +3 Punish Counter against Lights

raw.notes: Automatically triggers counterattack after 214K absorbs attack; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; can hold button to remain in Scutum stance after counterattack; hit advantage depends on attack that is absorbed; Marisa's short 6f hitstop causes attack to land as a +1 Counter-hit against most Medium/Heavy normals, while landing as a +3 Punish Counter against Lights

rows.name: Scutum (Counter)
rows.notes: Automatically triggers counterattack after 214KK absorbs attack; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; can hold button to remain in Scutum stance after counterattack; hit advantage depends on attack that is absorbed; Marisa's short 6f hitstop causes attack to land as a +1 Counter-hit against most Medium/Heavy normals, while landing as a +3 Punish Counter against Lights

raw.notes: Automatically triggers counterattack after 214KK absorbs attack; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; can hold button to remain in Scutum stance after counterattack; hit advantage depends on attack that is absorbed; Marisa's short 6f hitstop causes attack to land as a +1 Counter-hit against most Medium/Heavy normals, while landing as a +3 Punish Counter against Lights

rows.name: Tonitrus 1
rows.notes: 1st active frame only hits airborne opponents (functionally 8+16f startup vs. grounded); causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; hitconfirm window is 1f shorter vs. grounded opponents; cannot be canceled into Tonitrus 2 on whiff; range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

raw.notes: 1st active frame only hits airborne opponents (functionally 8+16f startup vs. grounded); causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; hitconfirm window is 1f shorter vs. grounded opponents; cannot be canceled into Tonitrus 2 on whiff; range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

rows.name: Tonitrus 2
rows.notes: True blockstring from overhead starter; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; cannot be performed when Tonitrus 1 whiffs

raw.notes: True blockstring from overhead starter; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; cannot be performed when Tonitrus 1 whiffs

rows.name: Procella
rows.notes: Risky low ender; may catch opponent by surprise since the overhead ender is safe on block; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

raw.notes: Risky low ender; may catch opponent by surprise since the overhead ender is safe on block; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

rows.name: Enfold
rows.notes: Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; Range: 1.109 (final active frame: 1.164); range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

raw.notes: Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; Range: 1.109 (final active frame: 1.164); range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

rows.name: Javelin of Marisa
rows.notes: Can hold button 27f for more damage and a cinematic counter that activates against strikes and throws

raw.notes: Can hold button 27f for more damage and a cinematic counter that activates against strikes and throws

rows.name: Javelin of Marisa
rows.notes: Held version; only the first 3 active frames trigger cinematic hit, which deals extra damage and Drive damage; max-range non-cinematic version does not give a Hard Knockdown; if frames 1-27 absorb a strike or throw, results in Counterattack version instead; has slightly less range than the uncharged version

raw.notes: Held version; only the first 3 active frames trigger cinematic hit, which deals extra damage and Drive damage; max-range non-cinematic version does not give a Hard Knockdown; if frames 1-27 absorb a strike or throw, results in Counterattack version instead; has slightly less range than the uncharged version

rows.name: Javelin of Marisa
rows.notes: Automatically triggers counterattack after Held SA1 armor absorbs a strike or throw on frames 1-27; can be baited with safe jump timing

raw.notes: Automatically triggers counterattack after Held SA1 armor absorbs a strike or throw on frames 1-27; can be baited with safe jump timing

rows.name: Meteorite
rows.notes: Useful anti-air, reversal, and juggle ender; can be steered by holding forward/back; this affects the range, allowing you to hold back for close anti-airs or hold forward in ranged juggles or fireball punishes; upward hit triggers a cinematic; on block/whiff, ends with a downward slam that does 500 less damage than the cinematic version (still does full Drive Damage on hit); Marisa's collision box extends far downward, preventing opponent from walking or dashing underneath (opponent can still jump to avoid 2nd hit)

raw.notes: Useful anti-air, reversal, and juggle ender; can be steered by holding forward/back; this affects the range, allowing you to hold back for close anti-airs or hold forward in ranged juggles or fireball punishes; upward hit triggers a cinematic; on block/whiff, ends with a downward slam that does 500 less damage than the cinematic version (still does full Drive Damage on hit); Marisa's collision box extends far downward, preventing opponent from walking or dashing underneath (opponent can still jump to avoid 2nd hit)

rows.name: Goddess of the Hunt
rows.notes: Takes opponent to corner; cinematic time regenerates ~2.1 Drive bars for Marisa; excellent whiff punish and projectile counter tool (especially with Modern controls)

raw.notes: Takes opponent to corner; cinematic time regenerates ~2.1 Drive bars for Marisa; excellent whiff punish and projectile counter tool (especially with Modern controls)

rows.name: Goddess of the Hunt (CA)
rows.notes: Takes opponent to corner; available at 25% HP or below; cinematic time regenerates ~2.1 Drive bars for Marisa; excellent whiff punish and projectile counter tool (especially with Modern controls)

raw.notes: Takes opponent to corner; available at 25% HP or below; cinematic time regenerates ~2.1 Drive bars for Marisa; excellent whiff punish and projectile counter tool (especially with Modern controls)

rows.name: Neutral Taunt
rows.notes: "I may need a second helping of you!"

raw.notes: "I may need a second helping of you!"

rows.name: Forward Taunt
rows.notes: "Come on, the first hit's free."; active frames refer to armored period

raw.notes: "Come on, the first hit's free."; active frames refer to armored period

rows.name: Back Taunt
rows.notes: "This will be fun! *crack*"

raw.notes: "This will be fun! *crack*"
